concerns.text: T012 implements semantic overlap constraints (lexical > 0.4, cosine < 0.3) but omits the FR-001/US-1 requirement to validate that Fine axes are derived from 'independent narrative observations' (not subsets of Coarse). The task lacks logic to verify the source of the input data, silently relaxing the tautology-prevention constraint.

T026 implements a rule-based metric based on 'sentiment/coherence alignment'. This contradicts FR-004 and US-3 Acceptance Scenario 3, which explicitly mandate a score based on 'keyword presence and sentiment alignment'. The task silently drops the 'keyword presence' requirement, weakening the evaluation metric.

T027 implements 'Judge Calibration' (Kappa > 0.6) but omits the FR-006 requirement to 'validate the generated Consistency Scores against an external Gold Standard dataset'. The task conflates Judge reliability with the system-level validation of consistency scores, dropping a critical acceptance criterion.

T030 aggregates scores and T031 selects the test (ANOVA/Friedman), but no task exists to actually EXECUTE the chosen statistical test and generate the final statistical output. This drops the core deliverable of FR-005 (performing the test) and US-3 (producing the analysis result).

T025 implements the Judge model for 1-5 Likert scoring but omits the FR-004/US-3 requirement to output a 'binary flag indicating if the response adhered to the target phase'. The task silently drops this specific output field required by the spec.

T019 mentions a 'regeneration loop' but does not explicitly task the implementation of the specific 'Generation Limit Exceeded' error handling logic (log error, proceed if >= 50, else mark invalid) required by the Edge Cases section and FR-002.
